methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $21M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Compound Eye is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jason Devitt and Haoyang Wang that has raised $20.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms at Series B/C stages—including Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B)—plus one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $39M. Peer-median analysis across the four most directly comparable same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. The substantial gap between methods reflects the early-stage capital deployed versus later-stage peer multiples in a capital-intensive sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $33M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $832M. The wide dispersion underscores material uncertainty around Compound Eye's relative competitive position, technology differentiation, and path to the revenue scale already achieved by mature peers. Resolution of these factors—particularly evidence of product traction and market adoption—will be critical to narrowing the valuation band at future rounds.
